ABOUT.TITLE: About Hail Municipality
ABOUT.TEXT: Hail Municipality seeks to provide high-quality services through digital transformation, sustainable urban development, and improving the quality of life. We work to give our community access to services easily and transparently, in line with the Kingdom's Vision 2030.

HEADER.TITLE: Municipal Services
HEADER.SUBTITLE: A suite of digital and civil services provided by the municipality to help our citizens and residents.

ITEMS.TITLE: Issuing a Building Permit
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: An electronic service available on the Balady platform that enables beneficiaries to apply for a Building Permit or a Fencing Permit.

ITEMS.TITLE: Issuing a Commercial License
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: An electronic service available on the Balady platform that allows users to commence commercial activities by issuing a Commercial License.

ITEMS.TITLE: Submitting a Report
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: An electronic service available on the Balady platform, allowing individuals to register a complaint or observation related to municipal services.

ITEMS.TITLE: Hygiene Management
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: Request cleaning services or report areas that need attention.

ITEMS.TITLE: Inquiry About Transactions Service
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: An electronic service available on the Balady platform, allowing beneficiaries to track administrative correspondence transactions recorded in the Murasilat system across the Ministry and its affiliated municipalities.

ITEMS.TITLE: Public Parks and Facilities
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: View public parks and facilities available to you.

HEADER.TITLE: Municipality News
HEADER.SUBTITLE: Check out the most prominent events, initiatives and achievements implemented by Hail Municipality.

ITEMS.TITLE: Hail Municipality implements 38 diverse community initiatives
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: During the third quarter of 2024, the Hail Region Municipality implemented 38 diverse community initiatives, as part of voluntary initiatives and programs aimed at developing the local community.

ITEMS.TITLE: Hail Municipality showcases best community practices to improve the urban landscape
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: The Hail Region Municipality reviewed best practices in community engagement to improve the urban landscape, upgrade services, and a number of initiatives it is implementing with the aim of enhancing the quality of life.

ITEMS.TITLE: The Prince of Hail receives the first semi-annual media report on completed projects
ITEMS.DESCRIPTION: His Highness Prince Abdulaziz bin Saad received the first semi-annual media report, which covers the completed projects of the region's secretariat in the city of Hail and its affiliated governorates.

steps.step1: Login to the National Single Sign-on portal via the Balady platform.
steps.step2: Select Construction License Services, then Issue Construction.
steps.step3: License Services, then the required municipality.
steps.step4: Select the engineering office, then enter the number and year of issuance of the building survey, and attach a copy of the deed.
steps.step5: Submit the request to the identified engineering office, then complete the information and attachments in the engineering office and send it to the municipality.
steps.step6: Verify the information of the request, attachments and approvals by the municipality.
steps.step7: Approve the request and issue a SADAD payment. Pay the fees and print the license.

PRINCES.TITLE: Region's Princes
PRINCES.INTRO: Princes have been appointed to Hail region through the following sequence:
PRINCES.PRINCE_1: On 18 Rajab 1438 AH, His Royal Highness Prince Abdulaziz bin Saad bin Abdulaziz Al Saud was appointed as Prince of Hail region until present
PRINCES.PRINCE_2: On 16 Sha'ban 1420 AH, His Royal Highness Prince Saud bin Abdulmohsen bin Abdulaziz was appointed as Prince of Hail region until 18 Rajab 1438 AH
PRINCES.PRINCE_3: On 2 Jumada al-Awwal 1400 AH, His Royal Highness Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz was appointed as Prince of Hail region until 16 Sha'ban 1420 AH
PRINCES.PRINCE_4: On 3 Sha'ban 1394 AH, Sheikh Nasser Abdullah bin Sheikh was delegated to act as Deputy Governor of Hail region and exercise the powers of the Prince
PRINCES.PRINCE_5: On 1 Sha'ban 1392 AH, His Highness Prince Saad bin Fahd Al Saud was appointed as Prince of Hail region
PRINCES.PRINCE_6: On 21 Muharram 1391 AH, His Highness Prince Fahd bin Saad bin Abdulrahman Al Saud was appointed as Prince of Hail region
PRINCES.PRINCE_7: In late 1341 AH, His Highness Prince Abdulaziz bin Musaed bin Jalawi Al Saud was appointed as Prince of Hail region until 1391 AH
PRINCES.PRINCE_8: On 29 Safar 1340 AH, Ibrahim bin Salim Al-Sabhan was appointed as Prince of Hail region

MAYORS.TITLE: Municipality Heads and Region's Mayors
MAYORS.INTRO: A group of heads have succeeded in heading the municipality (Hail Municipality) since its establishment until the present time, including official and acting heads as follows:
MAYORS.MAYOR_1: Engineer / Sultan bin Hamid Al-Zaidi from 1443/2/21 AH until present
MAYORS.MAYOR_2: Engineer / Ibrahim bin Saeed Abu Ras from 1433/7/2 AH until 1443/5/27
MAYORS.MAYOR_3: Engineer / Abdulaziz bin Ibrahim Al-Toub from 1428/8/12 AH until 1433/7/1 AH
MAYORS.MAYOR_4: Dr. / Abdulaziz bin Yusuf Al-Amar from 1425/1/30 AH until 1428/8/11 AH
MAYORS.MAYOR_5: Engineer / Jadae bin Nahar Al-Qahtani from 1409 AH until 1425/1/29 AH
MAYORS.MAYOR_6: Dr. / Mohammed bin Ibrahim Al-Mousa from 1404 AH until 1409 AH
MAYORS.MAYOR_7: Mr. / Ibrahim bin Abdullah Al-Bulaihi from 1396 AH until 1404 AH
MAYORS.MAYOR_8: Sulaiman Al-Mamdoh - Hamad Al-Mubarak - Abdullah Al-Sunaidi - Abdullah Al-Rashid - Hamad Al-Sagheer
MAYORS.MAYOR_9: Sheikh / Mamdouh bin Sulaiman Al-Ali (from 1381 AH until 1392/8/27 AH)

HAIL_AREA.TITLE: Hail Region
HAIL_AREA.CONTENT: The history of Hail region extends to prehistoric times, through the pre-Islamic era and Islamic periods, where it was one of the most fortified places in the Arabian Peninsula, which protected it from the invasion of the kings of Al-Hira in Iraq, as well as the Ghassanid kings in Syria, until the unification of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia in 1351 AH (1932 AD). Hail is associated with the famous legend embodied in the character of Hatim Al-Ta'i, known for his generous hospitality. Hail was also connected to the ancient historical routes of commercial caravans and pilgrims, and Hail has produced a number of poets and writers, and was visited by several well-known European travelers in the twentieth century despite the difficult and arduous journey to reach it. Hail is originally the name of the valley on which the city is located. It was formerly known as Al-Qaryah, or Qaryat Hail. With the passage of time, people neglected the name Al-Qaryah and it became known by its valley name, Hail. As for the reason for the naming, it was said that this valley, which is now known as Al-Adira', used to flow in ancient times and would separate the people of Aja and Salma. In 1340 AH, King Abdul Aziz - may God have mercy on him - annexed Hail to this great entity, and since that date, the region has been living in the blessing of security and stability, and it has grown and flourished to become one of the main regions of the Kingdom. Within the framework of the comprehensive modern renaissance that the Kingdom is witnessing, Hail region has achieved a high status in all aspects of economic, social, service and other life.

GEOGRAPHY.TITLE: Geographical Overview of Hail
GEOGRAPHY.LOCATION_TEXT: Hail region is one of the administrative regions of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, with its capital in Hail city. It is located in northern Najd in the northwest of the Kingdom and ranks seventh in area (120,000 km2) and eighth in population (731,147 in 2019) among the Kingdom's regions. Administratively, it is divided into 8 governorates in addition to the Hail Emirate, and is famous for the Aja and Salma mountains and the historical symbol of generosity, Hatim Al-Tai.
GEOGRAPHY.AREA_TEXT: The total area of Hail region is about 120,000 km2, which is 6.1% of the total area of the Kingdom, ranking seventh by area among the regions. Hail extends about 400 km from north to south and about 500 km from east to west. The total population according to the latest 2019 statistics is about 731,147, making it the eighth most populous region in the Kingdom.

VISION_MISSION.TITLE: Vision & Mission
VISION_MISSION.VISION_TEXT: Hail... The most beautiful. Community well-being, a sustainable environment, and global investment-attractive tourism.
VISION_MISSION.MISSION_TEXT: A creative, smart, and automated administration that achieves sustainability and quality of life, and provides leading municipal services.
PILLARS: Empowerment and Sustainability in Resource Utilization

MAIN_GOALS.TITLE: Main Goals
GOALS: Achieve the highest rates in the Global Cities Prosperity Index.
Enhance institutional performance and the competencies of employees and service providers.
Attract local investments and compete globally in the field of logistics services.
Optimize the use of tourism assets and support them to reach global competitiveness.
Develop the municipal work system and achieve full automation.
Support sustainability and achieve maximum efficiency in resource utilization.
Leverage modern and global technologies and expertise to support municipal projects (infrastructure).
Achieve comprehensive oversight to enhance the city's visual image and reduce visual distortion.
